title: The Spiral Continuum
author: Steven Saint
elevator_pitch: A 1606 atlas arrives at a Lisbon scholar's apartment with a tidemark that turns out to be a fingerprint. Across four hundred years and eight volumes, a thirteen-stone lattice, a Jesuit cartographer, and a generational line of women carrying a single question reach the moment the question is finally answered.

registers.name: Trilogy register
registers.description: Close third person, past tense, multi-POV. Long observational sentences punctuated by hard short ones. Holmes-style detail-first inference.

registers.name: Almeida memoir register
registers.description: Late-Almeida memoir frame + Jesuit period syntax + Holmes beats, rendered into modern English via translator's-note conceit. 1652 frame.

registers.name: Aurora memoir register
registers.description: Aurora Coutinho's first-person memoir dictated 1965–1967 from Cabeceiras de Basto. Plain-spoken Portuguese-tempo English, direct address to her granddaughter Mariana.

registers.name: Period-historical register
registers.description: Three-POV-across-90-years close-third (1755–1849). Joaninha child-sensory; Inácio Coimbra-Jesuit period syntax; Maria Inês long-keeper; Conceição rural-craftswoman.

reading_order_note: Books are published 1–8 in reading order. Roman-numeral series-position convention: Book 7 is Volume VIII (closing volume); Book 8 is Volume VII (interquel between trilogy and Book 7).
